ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING TRIAL SETTING PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A) IS CONTINUED FROM MAY-12-2026, AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT. 304 TO ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION ON MAY-12-2026, AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT. 502 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION DUE TO NEW JUDICIAL ASSIGNMENT WITH JUDGE VICTOR HWANG. (502)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 502, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A) AND 36.5. OPPOSITION FILED BY DEFENDANTS. REPLY FILED. THE TRIAL DATE IS AUGUST 31, 2026, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS SEPTEMBER 9, 2026. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS AUGUST 25, 2026. SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS SHALL BE BROUGHT ON REGULAR NOTICE PURSUANT TO THE RELEVANT PROVISIONS OF THE C.C.P., UNLESS THE PARTIES STIPULATE OTHERWISE. BEFORE A PARTY FILES AND SERVES A SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTION, IT MUST CONTACT THE CLERK TO MAKE A RESERVATION. TIME TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY NOT YET SERVED IS SHORTENED TO 20 DAYS. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR JULY 14, 2026, AT 9:00 A.M., IN DEPARTMENT 502. C. IF THE PARTIES AGREE THAT NO CMC IS NECESSARY, THEY SHALL NOTIFY THE COURT AT LEAST TWO COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. 11. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 27, 2026, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 305. PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED BY ALL UNLESS A PARTY IS EXCUSED FOR GOOD CAUSE. A REQUEST TO EXCUSE A PERSONAL APPEARANCE MUST BE SUBMITTED TO DEPARTMENT305@SFTC.ORG TWO WEEKS BEFORE THE MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM. COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER ON TRIAL SCHEDULE AND SUBMIT STIPUALTION AND (PROPOSED) ORDER. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED.
ASBESTOS MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR AUG-27-2026 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 305, PER ORDER OF THE COURT FILED MAY 12, 2026. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Set For Trial On Aug-31-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF - FOR TENTATIVE PURPOSES ONLY
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (mini minutes continues 2 of 2).

8. No later than May 26, 2026:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.
9. A further case management conference is set for July 14, 2026, at 9:00 a.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.
10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than July 20, 2026. Response to the settlement demand is due August 3, 2026. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department305@sftc.org via electronic mail on August 10, 2026.
11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, August 27, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 305. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department305@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Counsel to submit proposed order repeating the ruling verbatim. Counsel to meet and confer on schedule and submit stipulation and order to the court if they so choose. = (305/SKF).

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Trial Setting Preference Pursuant To C.C.P. 36(A)
Off Calendar May-06-2026 Continued To May-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 502

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Trial Setting Preference Pursuant To C.C.P. 36(A)
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday May 12, 2026, in Department 304, Line 1.
Appearances: Counsel Andrew Seitz of the Frost Law Firm for Plaintiff;
Counsel Julia Woods of Womble Bond Dickinson LLP for Defendant.

After the conclusion of oral argument, The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a) and 36.5. Opposition filed by Defendants Viking Group Inc and P.E O'Hair & Co. Joinder filed by Defendants: (1) DAP, Inc.k/n/a La Miradar Products Co, Inc; (2) Morse Tec LLC, (3) Sterling Wander LLLP; and (4) ZE Active Safety US Inc. Conditional non-opposition filed by Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company Inc. Untimely joined filed by Pep Boys Manny, Moe and Jack of California LLC. Reply filed.
"Where a party meets the requisite standard for calendar preference under [Code of Civil Procedure section 36] subdivision (a), preference must be granted. No weighing of interests is involved." (Fox v. Superior Court (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 529, 535 ["The standard under subdivision (a), unlike under subdivision (d), which is more specific and more rigorous, includes no requirement of a doctor's declaration. To the contrary, a motion under subdivision (a) may be supported by nothing more than an attorney's declaration based upon information and belief as to the medical diagnosis and prognosis of any party"]; see also, e.g., Miller v. Superior Court (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1200, 1204 [statute "grants a mandatory and absolute right to trial preference"]; Swaithes v. Superior Court (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 1082, 1085 [trial court "has no power to balance the different interests of opposing litigants in applying the provision"]; Koch-Ash v. Superior Court (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 689, 694 [section 36(a) "must be deemed to be mandatory and absolute" and "no discretion is left to trial courts."].)
1. The trial date is August 31, 2026, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is September 9, 2026.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 25, 2026.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is August 14, 2026.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is August 28, 2026.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 11, 2026.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(mini minutes continues 1 of 2, see next entry)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Feb-17-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances, Not contested, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant The Scotts Company LLC's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Ed Santos and Jackie Alameda; Matter not reported; Bailiff: Not Present. (D304/EPS)
